title: Fresh Crunchy Moroccan Lettuce Roast
description: A light, yeasty Moroccan dish that combines baby lettuce leaves with aromatic spices and a crunchy almond finish, all cooked in a traditional ttukbaegi earthenware pot.
instructions: Preheat the ttukbaegi on medium heat until it is hot to the touch.
Pat dry the baby lettuce leaves and season them with salt, pepper, cumin, paprika, garlic powder, and lemon zest.
Drizzle olive oil over the seasoned lettuce and toss to coat evenly.
Place the seasoned lettuce in the hot ttukbaegi and spread it out in an even layer.
Add the chicken stock around the lettuce, not on top, to create steam without soaking the leaves.
Cover the ttukbaegi with its lid and let the lettuce roast for 12 minutes, then remove the lid to allow steam to escape.
After 12 minutes, stir the lettuce gently, then return the lid and roast for an additional 8 minutes until the leaves are tender and the edges are crisp.
Fold in crushed almonds just before serving to preserve their crunch.
